message:
fix(btree): correct index seek on interior pages and resolve cursor borrow conflicts

The binary_search_index_interior method can now return Found on an exact
key match instead of treating it as unreachable.  seek_index_internal
handles this by short-circuiting with SeekResult::Found, and index_insert
descends to the right child's leftmost leaf so the new cell is always
written to a leaf page.

Several cursor navigation and mutation methods (move_next, move_prev,
index_insert, table_insert) are restructured to extract fields into
locals or clone StackEntry before calling &mut self helpers, eliminating
overlapping shared/mutable borrow conflicts on the cursor's stack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
